name: listener
summary: Side-effect handler that runs whenever a specific event is dispatched.
description: Creates an event listener and registers it in an initializer
problem: You want some logic (send a welcome email, update an analytics counter, invalidate a cache) to run whenever a specific event happens, but you do not want the dispatcher of that event to know or care about the side effect.
appliesWhen: Something else has already emitted an event you want to react to
The reaction is a side effect (notification, analytics, cache invalidation, downstream sync)
You want the event producer to stay decoupled from this consumer
Multiple listeners may want to react to the same event
avoidWhen: The behavior is part of the producing flow's core logic — call it directly
You actually need a request/response pattern — listeners are fire-and-forget
The reaction needs ordering guarantees relative to other listeners — use an explicit pipeline
There is no event yet — author the event first via phpnomad/event
examples: Send a welcome email when a user is created
Increment a counter when an order is placed
Invalidate the dashboard cache when a payout completes
Notify Slack when a deployment finishes
tradeoffs: One new file plus a registration entry. Decouples producers from consumers but adds indirection — debugging requires knowing what listens to a given event (use `phpnomad inspect:di` to see the wiring).
outputs: A {name} listener class implementing the appropriate Listener interface
A registration mapping the event class to this listener in the initializer
postApply: Implement the listener's handle method with the side-effect logic
If the event class does not exist yet, scaffold it with phpnomad/event

name.aiHint: Name should describe what the listener does, in imperative form. PascalCase. Not the event name — the action.

event.description: FQCN of the event class to listen to
event.aiHint: Use the fully qualified class name of an existing event. If the event does not exist yet, scaffold it first via phpnomad/event.

initializer.description: FQCN of the initializer to register in
initializer.aiHint: Pick the initializer that owns related listeners for this domain. If unsure, use the project's root initializer.
